But there's basically two types of SOPs in any company. There's company-specific SOPs and role-specific SOPs. So company-specific SOPs, what those are is getting you oriented with the software, like G Suite, Slack, CRM, course content, Facebook groups, all of that stuff. Understanding the mission, vision, and values of the company, getting you on the contract, understanding the product education, how to communicate with the team, ensuring you to people, all of that stuff. So that's a company-specific SOP. What that means is that every single person in the company will be going through that specific type of process. Now, there's also role-specific SOPs, which is specific to your sales role, be that a setter or a closer. Now, there's kind of general stuff. There's role-specific general, which is like anybody. This is just sort of general high-level stuff that everybody's going to have to go through. And then for each individual position, there's beginning of day, middle of day, and end of day SOPs. Now, because for sales, the big thing is that you're kind of doing more or less the same activities every single day. So the beginning of day SOPs are going to be like team meetings and stuff, how to show up each day for the team meetings, set projections, et cetera.
